blog_articles.title: Home Battery Storage 101: Is a Solar Battery Right for You?
blog_articles.excerpt: Learn how home battery storage works, typical costs, and when it makes sense to pair batteries with your solar system.
blog_articles.content: Home battery storage allows you to capture excess solar energy during the day and use it at night or during grid outages. In this guide, we cover how lithium-ion batteries work, typical system sizes for residential homes, and how battery storage interacts with net metering and time-of-use rates. We also outline key questions to ask when deciding if battery storage is right for your home.
blog_articles.author: Dana Lee

blog_articles.title: How to Size a Home Battery for Your Solar System
blog_articles.excerpt: A step-by-step walkthrough for choosing the right battery capacity based on your energy usage and backup needs.
blog_articles.content: Sizing a home battery for your solar system starts with understanding your critical loads and typical nightly usage. In this article, we explain how to read your electric bill, estimate kWh needs during an outage, and match that to common battery sizes. We also compare single-battery vs. multi-battery setups and discuss how battery storage can reduce time-of-use charges.
blog_articles.author: Alex Martinez

blog_articles.title: Battery Storage vs. Backup Generator: Which Is Better?
blog_articles.excerpt: Comparing battery storage and backup generators for home emergency power, including costs, noise, and maintenance.
blog_articles.content: When homeowners compare backup options, battery storage often competes with traditional gas or propane generators. This article compares upfront costs, long-term operating expenses, noise, maintenance requirements, and environmental impact. We also explain scenarios where combining battery storage with a small generator can be effective.
blog_articles.author: Jordan Kim

blog_articles.title: Solar Basics: How Residential Solar Works in 2026
blog_articles.excerpt: A clear overview of how modern residential solar systems work, from panels and inverters to monitoring apps.
blog_articles.content: Modern residential solar systems are more efficient and reliable than ever. This article walks through how sunlight becomes electricity, the role of inverters, and how your system connects to the grid. We also introduce key concepts like net metering, time-of-use billing, and typical system sizes for different home types.
blog_articles.author: Alex Martinez

blog_articles.title: 2026 Guide to Solar Incentives, Tax Credits, and Rebates
blog_articles.excerpt: See how federal, state, and utility incentives can lower the cost of going solar in 2026.
blog_articles.content: Solar incentives can reduce your upfront cost by 20–40% or more. In 2026, many homeowners can still claim the federal investment tax credit, along with state-level rebates and utility performance-based incentives. This guide explains each type of incentive, how they stack, and what paperwork is typically required.
blog_articles.author: Dana Lee

blog_articles.title: Comparing Solar Loans, Leases, PPAs, and Cash Purchases
blog_articles.excerpt: Understand the pros and cons of solar loans, leases, PPAs, and cash payments so you can choose the right option.
blog_articles.content: There are four main ways to pay for solar: cash, loans, leases, and power purchase agreements (PPAs). This article compares ownership, monthly payments, maintenance responsibilities, and long-term savings for each structure. We also cover how credit score and home equity can influence which financing options are available.
blog_articles.author: Jordan Kim

blog_articles.title: Microinverters vs. String Inverters: What Homeowners Should Know
blog_articles.excerpt: A practical comparison of microinverters and string inverters, including reliability, cost, and monitoring.
blog_articles.content: The inverter is the brain of your solar system. In this article, we compare microinverters and string inverters, including how each handles shade, system expansion, and monitoring. We also outline which roof types and utility rate structures tend to favor each technology.
blog_articles.author: Alex Martinez

blog_articles.title: Best Roof Types for Solar: Asphalt, Tile, Metal, and More
blog_articles.excerpt: See how different roof materials and slopes affect solar installation cost and performance.
blog_articles.content: Not all roofs are created equal when it comes to solar. We break down how asphalt shingles, clay tile, metal, and flat roofs impact installation methods, cost, and long-term durability. The article also covers special considerations for older roofs and when it makes sense to re-roof before installing solar.
blog_articles.author: Dana Lee

blog_articles.title: Net Metering Explained: How Solar Credits Work on Your Bill
blog_articles.excerpt: Understand how net metering credits your solar production and why policies differ by state and utility.
blog_articles.content: Net metering is the policy that determines how you are credited for excess solar energy sent back to the grid. This article explains common net metering structures, how credits roll over month to month, and what happens when policies change. We also give examples from several states to show how savings can vary.
blog_articles.author: Jordan Kim

blog_articles.title: Annual Solar Maintenance Checklist for Homeowners
blog_articles.excerpt: A simple checklist you can follow each year to keep your solar system performing at its best.
blog_articles.content: Solar systems are low-maintenance, but not no-maintenance. This checklist covers visual inspections, monitoring app checks, and when to schedule professional service. We also explain signs that your system may not be performing as expected and what warranties typically cover.
blog_articles.author: Alex Martinez

financing_plans.name: Legacy Solar Loan (Closed to New Customers)

incentive_programs.name: Arizona Residential Solar Tax Credit
incentive_programs.description: State income tax credit for a portion of residential solar installation costs in Arizona.
incentive_programs.eligibilityText: Available to Arizona homeowners who install qualifying residential solar energy systems on their primary residence.

incentive_programs.name: Arizona Solar Performance Incentive – Max Saver
incentive_programs.description: High-value performance-based incentive for residential solar production in select Arizona ZIP codes.
incentive_programs.eligibilityText: Available to residential customers who install new grid-tied solar PV systems and enroll in the state performance reporting program.

incentive_programs.name: Arizona Solar + Storage Rebate
incentive_programs.description: Upfront rebate for pairing eligible battery storage with a new or existing solar PV system.
incentive_programs.eligibilityText: Homeowners must install qualifying battery storage connected to a solar PV system and meet program sizing requirements.

incentive_programs.name: APS Residential Solar Rebate
incentive_programs.description: Utility rebate for APS customers who install new residential solar systems.
incentive_programs.eligibilityText: Must be an APS residential electric customer installing a qualifying grid-tied solar system.

incentive_programs.name: Federal Solar Investment Tax Credit (ITC)
incentive_programs.description: Federal income tax credit for a percentage of qualified solar PV installation costs.
incentive_programs.eligibilityText: Available to taxpayers who install eligible solar PV systems on residential properties they own in the United States.

incentive_programs.name: California Solar Incentive for Homeowners
incentive_programs.description: State-level incentive to reduce upfront costs of residential solar systems in select California regions.
incentive_programs.eligibilityText: California homeowners installing new grid-tied solar on existing homes may qualify, subject to income and system size limits.

incentive_programs.name: Texas Residential Solar Property Tax Exemption
incentive_programs.description: State exemption so added home value from solar is not subject to property tax.
incentive_programs.eligibilityText: Applies to Texas homeowners who install qualifying solar energy devices on their property.

incentive_programs.name: Austin Energy Residential Solar Rebate
incentive_programs.description: Upfront rebate for qualifying residential solar installs within Austin Energy territory.
incentive_programs.eligibilityText: Must be an Austin Energy customer and complete the required solar education course before installation.

incentive_programs.name: Legacy Arizona Solar Bonus Rebate (Expired)
incentive_programs.description: A previous state rebate program for early adopters of residential solar in Arizona.
